mechanism_summary: I2S populates a large dictionary with all Ragel switch case constants; uniform draws from this dictionary lock vpc's corpus into whichever sister arm (3 or 5) gains residency first, crowding out the case-9 target arm.
one_line: I2S dictionary roulette across K switch arms crowds out target arm

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace observes three byte!=0 comparisons and substitutes the constant 0 into all three payload positions, steering vpc's corpus toward the all-zeros configuration that satisfies the blocked FALSE side.
one_line: I2S substitutes zero constant into all three byte positions, blocking TRUE side

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace observes the full CDATA literal chain and keeps re-substituting 'D' and surrounding bytes into mutated children, creating a one-way attractor toward the matched TRUE side and stranding vpc away from the needed FALSE-side non-D byte.
one_line: I2S attractor substitutes CDATA literal back, preventing non-D byte diversity

mechanism_summary: The 2-bit ECN gate is too narrow for I2S substitution to help; vpc pays overhead from I2S corpus pollution at many unrelated CMP sites across the OpenThread codepath, displacing CMP_MAP-driven havoc on the IPv6 header byte.
one_line: I2S overhead at unrelated CMPs displaces gradient exploration of 2-bit ECN field

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace intercepts scheme/header strcmp CMPs and substitutes well-formed operands into the input, actively repairing percent-escapes that naive's random havoc would leave malformed, steering cmplog away from the decode-failure state the branch requires.
one_line: I2S repairs malformed percent-escapes, steering away from decode-failure branch

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace floods cmplog's corpus with repeating operand bytes from unrelated harfbuzz shaper CMPs, overwriting the structured ASCII-plus-header inputs that naive's havoc retains; the resulting corpus never produces a Repha-category leading glyph.
one_line: I2S corpus pollution with integer CMP operands destroys structured header seeds needed for Repha glyph

mechanism_summary: I2S sees chunk-magic and CRC CMPs but not the runtime-computed prefix_length; satisfying those well-formed chunk literals steers inputs toward valid keywords (length 1-79), actively avoiding the out-of-range condition the branch checks.
one_line: I2S satisfies chunk magic CMPs, keeping keyword length inside valid range and blocking the malformed-length branch

mechanism_summary: I2SRandReplace anchors cmplog's corpus on file-extension and keyword literal CMP operands from the DOCTYPE/SYSTEM parser path, producing shallow inputs that never build a deep element tree; the runtime counter freeElemsNr never accumulates past MAX_FREE_NODES.
one_line: I2S anchors corpus on shallow DOCTYPE keyword inputs, starving deep-tree traversal needed to exceed counter cap

mechanism_summary: The blocker compares a runtime pointer against nullptr, not an input-derived value; cmplog's I2S substitution injects pointer-shaped bytes from surrounding LinkedList traversal CMPs, wasting mutation budget on bytes irrelevant to the protocol-level sequencing needed to populate and then remove a list entry.
one_line: I2S injects irrelevant pointer-byte substitutions, wasting budget on non-input-derived nullptr check

mechanism_summary: I2S substitutions of font/script/language tag literals from unrelated CMP sites bias both cmplog and vpc corpora toward inputs that always place a consonant at syllable start, so base always equals start and the Myanmar reordering loop never enters its true branch.
one_line: I2S tag-literal substitutions bias corpus so Myanmar syllable always starts with consonant, blocking loop entry

mechanism_summary: I2S rewrites seed bytes with canonical XML literal operands (xml, version, DOCTYPE, SYSTEM), anchoring the corpus on well-formed names that produce few distinct hash-bucket collisions, so the runtime collision counter nbi never exceeds MAX_HASH_LEN.
one_line: I2S anchors corpus on canonical XML literals, preventing diverse names needed to overflow hash-bucket collision counter
